purpose: Records the analysis-only research pass over Robinhood's newsroom design category. For each article, captures the lessons Robinhood describes and maps each lesson to Owlory surfaces with explicit applicability ratings plus non-applicable callouts. Does not propose implementation; may queue follow-up slices.
fetch_method: WebFetch + WebSearch to resolve canonical URLs (the index page is JavaScript-rendered so individual article hrefs are not visible in raw HTML; resolved via site search).
ratings_scale: high (clear direct applicability), medium (transferable principle but Owlory's context differs), low (marginally relevant), not-applicable (Owlory's context excludes it).
owlory_context_constraints: Owlory is a local-first, single-user iOS life-command-center; no broker, no money flow, no market events.
No marketing channel; the in-app experience IS the only surface.
Single-developer team; no agency partner, no parallel creative team.
Restrained Apple-default motion; uses SwiftUI primitives + DesignSystem helpers and L() for localization.
19 supported locales; localized content drift is a real concern (drift-check tool exists at Tools/localization-review-drift-check.py).

articles.title: Introducing a New Visual Identity Reflecting Robinhood's Growth and Vision for the Future
lessons: 'Less is more' as the guiding ethos — bold and sophisticated without distraction.
Foundation = black/white/neutrals; accent = a single bright tone (Robin Neon) used as purposeful pops.
Intentional modular layout system across app, web, email, social.
Streamlined, distraction-free experience so the user can focus on decisions.
Avoid imitating competitor patterns.

owlory_applicability.rationale: Already minimal/calm; the 'less is more' lesson validates current direction. Verify no future Today surface adds decorative noise.
Owlory already uses brandPrimary as purposeful accent over neutrals. Aligns with the 'purposeful pops' pattern.
Owlory has SwiftUI helpers but no formal layout-token doc. Could write a tokens.md to formalize spacing/typography/color rules.
Owlory is a life-command-center; no investor framing.
No direct competitor pattern to avoid; Owlory's niche is private/local-first life org, not a crowded category.

articles.title: Creative Spark: The Making of Robinhood's Gold Campaign
lessons: Reject visual maximalism that causes cognitive overload (rejected the 'editorial poster' explorations).
Soft mid-tone textures > high-contrast metallics for legibility.
Animation rhythm: a quick action followed by subtle floating motion.
3D direction-setting should happen earlier in the process to avoid two teams iterating in parallel and converging late.

owlory_applicability.rationale: Owlory ships in-app only; no TV, no balloons, no 3D campaign work.
No advertising channel; no agency.
The 'avoid maximalism via cognitive overload' lesson maps to dashboard surfaces where stacking more cards/sections is tempting. Reinforces minimalism.

Adding decorative motion would contradict article-1's 'less is more.' Owlory's current Apple-default motion is the right baseline. Specific completion micro-feedback (haptic + subtle scale) is already adequate.

The 'set direction earlier to avoid siloed parallel work' lesson maps directly to project_multi_agent_collisions. The supervisor + slice queue is Owlory's existing answer. Validates current direction.

articles.title: Design Smarter, Not Harder: The Power of Integrated Content Standards
lessons: Move content standards INTO the design system rather than keep them in a separate doc. Robinhood integrated content guidance into their Bento design system.
Categorize guidance per component: best practices, mechanics, content length/structure, Do's/Don'ts.
Component-specific standards: error messages, CTAs, push notifications, alerts, modals.
Anti-pattern: vague error messages like 'Error code: 1234' — every error should include specific resolution guidance.
Anti-pattern: inconsistent capitalization (mixed title-case/sentence-case buttons), inconsistent header punctuation.
Anti-pattern: using the wrong component type for a given content type.

owlory_applicability.rationale: Owlory's L() + Localizable.strings is the content layer. Content guidance currently lives in docs/workflows/localization-translation-quality.md, separate from any visual token doc. Integrating CTA copy / error phrasing / header capitalization guidance into a single content+design reference would close this gap.

owlory_applicability.surface: Error messaging across stores (TrainStore.lastError, WriteStore.lastError, etc.)
owlory_applicability.rationale: Multiple stores expose lastError as a String; some may be runtime exception messages without resolution guidance. Worth a follow-up audit slice that ensures each error has a localized resolution-guided message.

owlory_applicability.surface: CTA copy (Save, Cancel, Continue, Check-in, primary actions)
owlory_applicability.rationale: Owlory has many primary actions; standardizing CTA capitalization and verb choice across surfaces would reduce translation drift across 19 locales.
Title-case vs sentence-case across en source could propagate to 19 locales' translations. Worth recording a one-pager convention so future English additions stay consistent.
Already in this spirit — automated content guardrails. Could be paired with a capitalization-consistency check or CTA-vocabulary check as a follow-up.

articles.title: Winding Career Paths: How You Got to Robinhood
lessons: Article is primarily biographical/recruiting content. Minimal concrete design methodology.
Generic principles surfaced: human-centered focus, data-informed iteration, ownership mindset, portfolio-driven growth.

owlory_applicability.rationale: Bio article rather than design-instructive. Generic principles (human-centered, data-informed) are too broad to drive specific Owlory decisions. No follow-up recommended.

articles.title: Robinhood Retirement through the eyes of Research, Creative and Design
lessons: Make complex products 'approachable, digestible and purpose-built' rather than complex/boring.
Generative research embedded within evaluative sessions for efficiency.
Visual concept: 'perpetual motion machines' to convey how money behaves when working for you. Typography/messaging/diagrams/charts all reinforce the same metaphor.
Plug-and-play design systems for non-linear customer discovery paths (in-app vs marketing vs social).
Daily jam sessions as a cohesive user-centric unit.
Research-backed messaging when presenting to senior stakeholders.
Avoid overly complex and boring financial products.
Don't separate marketing from product experience; blur intentionally.

owlory_applicability.surface: Owlory's overall ethos (life command-center for individuals)
owlory_applicability.rationale: 'Approachable, digestible, purpose-built' validates Owlory's existing direction. Today/Train/Write/Home/Career surfaces are intentionally small-and-clear rather than feature-dense.

Non-linear discovery — a user can arrive at a Train session via Today's Continue row, via the Train tab, or via a focus suggestion. 'Plug-and-play' maps directly to the ContinueSubtitleKind + FocusSuggestionRules.Reason semantic data types already shipping. Validates the existing structural refactor pattern.

Robinhood's 'money in motion' is a unifying metaphor. Owlory has implicit narratives ('Today', 'Continue', 'momentum from Patterns digests') but no formal named metaphor. Could optionally write a brief design-vision doc that names the throughline (e.g., 'quiet progress' or 'gentle momentum').

'Daily jam sessions as cohesive user-centric unit' has no direct parallel in Owlory (single developer + agents), but the supervisor/queue model functions as the asynchronous equivalent. Validates existing model.
Owlory has no marketing channel; the in-app experience is the only surface. There's nothing to 'blur' across.

owlory_applicability.surface: Retirement-specific research methods (lit review, campus intercepts)
owlory_applicability.rationale: No retirement domain, no field research budget. The 'generative-within-evaluative' efficiency tip is interesting but Owlory's research is single-developer informal observation.

dominant_lessons: Non-linear discovery via plug-and-play semantic types (#5)

DesignSystem + L() + Localizable.strings.validates_current_direction: partly — DesignSystem and L() exist but there is no single integrated content-and-design reference
new_action_items: Optional follow-up: write a docs/workflows/content-standards.md (or merge into existing localization-translation-quality.md) covering CTA capitalization, error-message phrasing, header punctuation conventions, and component-to-content-type mapping.
Optional follow-up: inventory Owlory's lastError strings and runtime error surfaces; ensure each provides a localized resolution-guided message rather than a raw exception description.

Visual narrative / motion.validates_current_direction: implicitly — Owlory has 'Today', 'Continue', 'momentum from Patterns' but no formally named metaphor
new_action_items: Optional follow-up: short docs/decisions/0001-design-vision-metaphor.md naming the throughline that connects Today/Continue/Patterns/Train consistency. Do NOT add decorative motion that would contradict 'less is more' from #1.

dominant_lessons: Set direction earlier to avoid siloed parallel work (#2)

not_applicable_summary: Fintech-specific framing (broker, retirement product, market events) — Owlory has none of these.
Marketing/campaign creative (TV, 3D, agency partnerships) — Owlory ships in-app only.
Field research at scale (campus intercepts, retirement lit review) — Owlory has no research budget; informal observation only.
Real-money UX patterns (APY display, balance hierarchy, regulatory disclaimers) — Owlory does not handle money.

follow_up_slice_recommendations.rationale: Article #3 is the most actionable. Owlory's content-design layer (L() + Localizable.strings) and visual-design layer (DesignSystem) exist separately. A single integrated reference covering CTA capitalization, error-message phrasing, header punctuation, and component-to-content-type mapping would prevent translation drift and reduce decision overhead for future surfaces.
follow_up_slice_recommendations.estimated_size: doc-only, 4-6 files (new content-standards.md + cross-links from existing docs)

follow_up_slice_recommendations.rationale: Article #3's anti-pattern 'Error code: 1234' style. Audit Owlory's lastError surfaces across TrainStore, WriteStore, HomeStore, CareerStore, TodayStore for vague exception messages without resolution guidance.
follow_up_slice_recommendations.estimated_size: audit-only, may queue per-store fix slices

follow_up_slice_recommendations.rationale: Article #5's 'perpetual motion machines' as unifying metaphor. Owlory has implicit narratives; an ADR that names the throughline (Today/Continue/Patterns/Train consistency) would guide future surfaces. Optional — only if the project-owner wants to formalize.

not_claimed: Robinhood's design system is directly portable to Owlory (it is not; very different product and team scale).
Any of the queued follow-up recommendations should be implemented (the project-owner decides which, if any).
All five articles are equally instructive (article #4 is biographical with minimal design content; honest rating: low/none).
Robinhood's specific tooling (Bento, Cinema 4D, Figma component variables) maps to Owlory's toolchain (it does not).
